LABEL: corona radiata
Definition: A granulosa cell layer forming the innermost stratum of the cumulus oophorus, composed of columnar granulosa cells directly apposed to the zona pellucida of the oocyte, which adhere to the oocyte prior to ovulation and must be dispersed by hyaluronidase from the sperm acrosome to permit fertilization.

LABEL: corpus luteum granulosa lutein layer
Definition: A cell layer that forms the inner zone of the corpus luteum, composed of large luteal cells (granulosa lutein cells) derived from luteinized follicular granulosa cells, and that produces oestrogens via aromatase activity using androgens synthesized by the surrounding theca lutein layer.

LABEL: corpus luteum granulosa theca layer
Definition: A cellular layer of the corpus luteum comprising both granulosa-lutein cells (large luteal cells derived from follicular granulosa cells, located centrally and arranged in cords around vascular spaces) and theca-lutein cells (small luteal cells derived from theca interna cells, located peripherally between the folds of the granulosa-lutein cell layer), which together form the principal steroidogenic parenchyma of the corpus luteum and cooperate in the biosynthesis of progesterone and estrogen.

LABEL: cumulus oophorus oocyte complex
Definition: A reproductive structure consisting of the oocyte together with its surrounding cumulus oophorus cells within the antral follicle, interconnected by gap junctions that facilitate bidirectional communication essential for oocyte maturation and competence prior to ovulation.

LABEL: dominance antral follicle
Definition: A tertiary ovarian follicle that has been selected from the cohort of antral follicles during the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle, characterised by its ability to suppress the growth of co-developing follicles through increased oestradiol and inhibin secretion, and destined to reach full maturity and ovulate.

LABEL: early antral follicle
Definition: A tertiary ovarian follicle in the earliest stage of antrum formation, typically 2-5 mm in diameter, characterized by a small fluid-filled antral cavity, active granulosa cell proliferation under FSH stimulation, and belonging to the recruited cohort that enters the late luteal phase of the preceding menstrual cycle prior to follicle selection.

LABEL: hemorrhagic anovulatory follicle
Definition: An ovarian follicle that fails to ovulate and undergoes intrafollicular hemorrhage, resulting in the antral cavity becoming filled with blood rather than follicular fluid, without rupture or formation of a corpus luteum.

LABEL: luteinized unruptured follicle
Definition: An ovarian follicle that has undergone luteinization following the mid-cycle LH surge but has failed to rupture and release the oocyte, resulting in progesterone secretion by the granulosa-lutein cells in the absence of ovulation.

LABEL: multilayer ovarian follicle
Definition: A preantral ovarian follicle in which the granulosa cells have proliferated to form multiple complete concentric layers surrounding the oocyte, representing the most advanced preantral stage immediately preceding antrum formation.

LABEL: pre-selection antral follicle
Definition: A tertiary ovarian follicle that has grown beyond the early antral stage but has not yet been selected as the dominant follicle, typically 5-10 mm in diameter, competing with cohort members for FSH support during the early follicular phase and exhibiting increasing estrogen secretion and FSH receptor expression.

LABEL: selection antral follicle
Definition: A tertiary ovarian follicle that has been selected from the recruited cohort to become the dominant follicle, typically greater than 10 mm in diameter, distinguished by its enhanced sensitivity to FSH, accelerated growth rate of approximately 1.5 mm per day, and capacity to suppress the development of remaining cohort follicles through inhibin and estrogen secretion.

LABEL: transitional primary ovarian follicle
Definition: A preantral ovarian follicle at an intermediate developmental stage between the primary follicle and the secondary follicle, characterized by granulosa cells forming more than one but not yet multiple complete layers around the oocyte.

LABEL: transitional primordial ovarian follicle
Definition: An ovarian follicle at an intermediate developmental stage between the primordial and primary follicle stages, distinguished by a mixture of flat squamous and cuboidal granulosa cells surrounding the oocyte.
